questions.question: The number of moles of solute in 4 Liters of a 0.472 M solution is:
The number of moles of solute in 2 Liters of a 0.498 M solution is:
At 418 K, the equilibrium constant Kp for a reaction is 7. If the temperature is increased, favoring the endothermic direction, what happens to Kp?
questions.solution: For endothermic reactions, increasing temperature increases the equilibrium constant.

questions.question: Which of the following alkyl halides undergoes SN1 reaction most rapidly under identical conditions? (Solvent: aqueous ethanol, 59°C)
questions.hint: SN1 mechanisms proceed via carbocation intermediates. The more stable the carbocation, the faster the reaction.
questions.solution: SN1 rate depends on carbocation stability (3° > 2° > 1°). Option A forms a highly stable tertiary carbocation.
